title: Evodiopanax malayanus
taxonomy: Evodiopanax malayanus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This family is commonly known as the ginseng or ivy family. The plant is native to Malaysia and is also found in other parts of Southeast Asia. Its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the order Apiales. The specific classification is currently accepted by the botanical community.
uses: Evodiopanax malayanus is known for its various medicinal uses. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asia to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments due to its purported anti-inflammatory properties.
